name: Aargau (Switzerland)
description: A square flag divided vertically: the hoist half black with three white wavy bars (the river Aare), the fly half blue with three white five-pointed stars.
history: An original 1803 design by Samuel Ringier-Seelmatter for the new canton of the Helvetic Republic; the stars were specified as five-pointed in 1930.

features.symbolism: per pale: black at the hoist, blue at the fly

features.role: three white wavy bars representing the river Aare
